name: Retained 78-prompt regression set
purpose: Regression evaluation only. Never use these prompts as hardcoded routing or answer rules.

conversations.title: QA Retest 1 - Qualification
prompts: Hey there! Hope you’re doing well—can you help me with a few qualification questions?
Are we equating social media presence or follower count with success when evaluating applicants? How much weight should we give social media versus the overall business, story, and fit?
I have a Call 1 for Operation CEO, and the applicant has two different businesses. Should they choose one business to focus on, or can they mention both during the episode?
An author has a successful book, a corresponding podcast, and a screenplay gaining traction, but no official business. Is that a fit for America’s Authors if they have a website for the book?
Is a franchise owner eligible for Next Level CEO?
Should a non-veteran be auditioning for Operation CEO if their business partner is a veteran and they have five employees?
A lead says their website, social media, and company have not launched yet and they are in the very early stages. Should I still conduct Call 1 or tell them now that they are not a fit?
Do we have a live episode on the network featuring someone who owns or runs a charity or nonprofit that I can send to a potential cast member?
Are we still running Kingdom Creators?

conversations.title: QA Retest 2 - Payments Rights
prompts: Thanks. I’m switching to payments, contracts, and content rights now.
Why is there a commercial reuse license? If a cast member declines the reuse license, does being greenlit mean we can use their segment but they cannot reuse the content?
Clients understand that they own their episode with the license, but some ask, “How come I don’t make money from the residuals?”
Are the promotional views included in the $20K package for Facebook only?
With the 100K guaranteed trailer views, does that mean 100K clicks or 100K views of the trailer all the way to the end? How long is the trailer?

If someone splits $20K into four payments and makes the first payment by ACH, will the remaining payments draft automatically by ACH? Do they need to approve each payment, or do we need a credit card for future payments?

A client paid through the link by ACH, but the contract did not appear; it only said “ACH in progress.” I sent the agreement separately, and the client says they signed it. Where can I see the signed agreement?
I have the signed contract, but the client paid by ACH. Do I need to wait for the payment to clear before signing them up for onboarding?
My lead wants the $20K license but wants to make a $3K first payment now and pay the remaining $17K in three weeks. Which contract should they sign?

conversations.title: QA Retest 3 - Calls Leads
prompts: Appreciate it. Next I have questions about calls, greenlights, recordings, and lead handling.
Are we allowed to send the payment link through a Zoom Phone message if the lead had to leave Call 2 and wants to complete payment afterward?
A client is trying to pay on their phone, but the payment page will not let them tap the button to agree to the terms. I sent backup links, and the same thing happens. What should I do?
When Call 1 happens on a Friday, are we supposed to schedule Call 2 for Monday, or should it happen before the Sunday cohort closes?
If a prospect says they already read the greenlight letter, do we still need to walk them through it on the call?
Am I allowed to use Fathom and Zoom recording at the same time, using Zoom as the backup and Fathom for transcripts, summaries, coaching feedback, action items, and call review?
When dialing out on Zoom for the 20% list, do we have to record the calls? If so, do we have to tell prospects they are being recorded?

For self-sourced clients, what is the proper process for getting them through the application and onto my own calendar? How do I make sure the application is attributed to me instead of routed to the general pool?

A lead originally applied for Legacy Makers but canceled Call 2 because they could not make the investment. They are now asking about America’s Authors. Since it is a different show, must they wait three to six months, or can they apply now?

conversations.title: QA Retest 4 - Production Events
prompts: Last section—proof, production, support, platforms, and events. Thank you for sticking with me.

For Next Level CEO, our morning confirmation text says we cannot rebook because the cohort closes Sunday, but DJ has no cohort. Can I change that wording myself, or should I leave it unchanged until the team updates it?
Where can I find a client’s show name if it is missing from their Keap profile and is no longer on my calendar?

A potential cast member asked whether I can send the 2025 Inside Success statistics slide, including the social reach and combined following. Can I send a screenshot, or should I only reference the information in a message?
Are the podcast questions designed for lead generation? What is the podcast episode structure, and how is it intelligently designed?
Do we have any additional material that explains the six-month training program really well?
On the fulfillment side, are cast members still paired with a scriptwriter? If a cast member is filming in August, when will they receive the script?
Can a cast member choose which Tier 1 streaming platform their show will be submitted to?
Is the Mastermind only once a year? Are there other in-person training and networking programs throughout the year, or just the one event?
Thanks—can you make that last answer shorter?
Perfect, thank you!

conversations.title: QA Retest 5 - Qualification Natural
prompts: Hi! How’s your day going?
I have several unrelated sales questions. Can you keep your answers short and practical?
For Operation CEO, must the applicant own an LLC, or can the owner of a nonprofit qualify?
Can you explain that in simpler language?
If a prospect is early-stage and budget-sensitive, may I start by recommending Lite, or must I present the higher package first and then move down?
Can a Next Level CEO applicant be greenlit if the business officially launches in three months but they already have a website, a strong story, and the ability to invest?
For America’s Top Agents, does a real-estate agent need written brokerage legal or compliance approval before moving forward?
Should freelancers move to Call 2, or do they need an established business to qualify?
Is the $5,000 CEO Day upgrade currently available for Next Level CEO? If it is available, what payment and turnaround terms apply?
That’s helpful. Can you give me the answer without repeating the route note?
If someone is already an ISTV customer but applies for a different ISTV show, should I proceed with the new application or skip the call?

conversations.title: QA Retest 6 - Payments Context
prompts: Thanks. I’m switching to payments and contracts now.
If a business partner who is not in Keap pays on behalf of the cast member, how should I confirm the payment and get the contract signed if it does not populate automatically?
I’m not sure which show this applies to. What information do you need from me?
Do I need to submit another PayMe entry when a recurring payment appears in the payment feed?
How do I verify that a Next Level CEO contract has been signed?
Actually, my previous question was about main ISTV, not Next Level CEO. Does that change your answer?
Do reps send invoices for recurring client payments, or is that automated? Should those payments appear in the sales ledger?
What was my previous question about?
How does the first Next Level CEO payment appear on the receipt for tax write-off purposes?
What discount is currently available to a VIP client who purchases a second VIP ISTV episode, and where can I find the list of included media outlets?

conversations.title: QA Retest 7 - Calls Compliance
prompts: Appreciate it. Now I have some questions about calls, ownership, and compliance.
If my public calendar only allows bookings within two days but an outbound lead is available next week, what is the correct booking process?
What should I do if a prospect starts taking photos of the Call 1 presentation slides during the call?
You misunderstood me—I’m asking what the rep should do, not what the prospect should do.
Can I cancel an audition for someone I recently disqualified who keeps applying again, and how should I stop repeat bookings?
If a client opts out and I can no longer text them in Zoom Phone or email them through Keap, how do I complete the opt-out process?
What email and text wording should I use the night before and the morning of a call booked from the 20% list?
If a client books calls with two different reps, which rep owns the client?
If a dial-out lead no-showed Call 1 with another rep, may I rebook the lead on my calendar, or should I contact the original rep?

conversations.title: QA Retest 8 - Production Resources
prompts: Last section—I have questions about production, resources, platforms, and events.
Does the estimated nine-month timeline for appearing on Amazon Prime begin when the client signs the license or when they film at the studio? When should the episode appear on our own platform?
What is the current studio address, and is the Mastermind held at that location?
What social promotional assets are included in the $10,000 Next Level CEO package, and what should I avoid promising?
Can a prospect’s friends tour the studio before the shoot, and how many guests may attend filming?
Where can I find the document that explains the complete production process, including pre-production, filming, post-production, and expected scheduling timelines?
Please turn your previous answer into a short checklist.
Is there approved training for transferring or managing leads between Keap and HubSpot, or should reps avoid doing that themselves?
Should details about event access be explained during Call 2 or during onboarding after the sale?
Thanks, that’s everything for now.
